pr_title: consistency2: governance constraints + post-bake-off plan reconciliation
title: plans/okay-i-want-you-breezy-duckling.md` (lines 30–35) embeds a hardcoded Infisical project ID (`9ec75f86-d604-4cfa-902c-57cb1e372adc`) and a production Logfire base URL in a bash snippet. The token
body:
`plans/okay-i-want-you-breezy-duckling.md` (lines 30–35) embeds a hardcoded Infisical project ID (`9ec75f86-d604-4cfa-902c-57cb1e372adc`) and a production Logfire base URL in a bash snippet. The token is fetched at runtime, but the project ID and URL are now in git history permanently. Per the project Secrets Management rule, access parameters belong in Doppler, not committed docs.

**Action:** Redact the project ID/URL, OR add `plans/` to `.gitignore` now (the PR description already flags this — gitignoring is the cleaner fix since plan-mode artifacts are ephemeral).

---

pr_title: consistency2: governance constraints + post-bake-off plan reconciliation
title: plans/bright-dazzling-yeti.md`, `plans/scalable-mixing-naur.md`, and `plans/swift-juggling-wadler.md` are byte-identical (md5: `75dcc8e44660b54ef212d9f43ebfe2a2`). All three are 315-line copies of the
body:
`plans/bright-dazzling-yeti.md`, `plans/scalable-mixing-naur.md`, and `plans/swift-juggling-wadler.md` are byte-identical (md5: `75dcc8e44660b54ef212d9f43ebfe2a2`). All three are 315-line copies of the same Stage 3 planning content. A more complete 487-line variant exists at `plans/okay-this-all-makes-idempotent-pretzel.md`.

Per the **Sweep when you fix** rule: committing three byte-identical files establishes this as the preferred pattern. Delete the two redundant copies (`scalable-mixing-naur`, `swift-juggling-wadler`) before merge, or add `plans/` to `.gitignore` and remove the directory.

---

`CLAUDE.md` retains both in full. A Codex agent encountering `sessions.ts` importing drizzle-orm during a cleanup phase cannot confirm it is intentional — it may flag it or try to fix it and break the route.

pr_title: consistency2: governance constraints + post-bake-off plan reconciliation
title: The `command -v doppler` guard at line 73 runs **before** the `NOTION_API_KEY` env-var check at line 87. Any Archon node that injects `NOTION_API_KEY` into the environment but runs without the doppler
body: The `command -v doppler` guard at line 73 runs **before** the `NOTION_API_KEY` env-var check at line 87. Any Archon node that injects `NOTION_API_KEY` into the environment but runs without the doppler binary will still fail at line 73. The env-var path exists but is unreachable on non-doppler machines. Inline comment filed.

pr_title: fix(archon): expand brace shorthand in cleanup-extract.sh + PR-18 plan closure
title: HTML dispatch graph is stale on arrival** (`docs/audit/pr-dispatch-graph.html`): The file shows PR-18 as `inprog` in both the wave card (line 288) and the status table (line 481), but the `cleanup-pla
body: **HTML dispatch graph is stale on arrival** (`docs/audit/pr-dispatch-graph.html`): The file shows PR-18 as `inprog` in both the wave card (line 288) and the status table (line 481), but the `cleanup-plan.md` changes in this same PR mark PR-18 as done (→ PR #231). The HTML was generated before the closure and not regenerated. Merging this creates an immediately-wrong snapshot in the repo. Either regenerate the HTML to reflect the done state, or note in the PR body that the HTML is intentionally a historical snapshot.

pr_title: fix(archon): expand brace shorthand in cleanup-extract.sh + PR-18 plan closure
title: Unquoted `$options` in `for` loop is subject to pathname expansion** (`.archon/scripts/cleanup-extract.sh`): With `IFS=','`, bash word-splits on commas correctly — but it also performs glob expansion
body:
**Unquoted `$options` in `for` loop is subject to pathname expansion** (`.archon/scripts/cleanup-extract.sh`): With `IFS=','`, bash word-splits on commas correctly — but it also performs glob expansion on each split token before the loop body runs. A path segment containing `*` or `[` would expand against the working directory. Safer alternative:

```bash
# Instead of:
local IFS=','
for opt in $options; do

# Use read -ra (prevents globbing, same IFS-split behavior):
local IFS=','
read -ra _opts <<< "$options"
for opt in "${_opts[@]}"; do
```

pr_title: fix(session): stabilize core learning session flow
title: File:** `.test-receipts/mobile.json
body:
**File:** `.test-receipts/mobile.json`

The `command` field in the receipt is a manually composed jest invocation with `--runTestsByPath` restricting the run to a hand-picked subset of files. `record-test-receipt.sh` **always** writes `command: "pnpm exec nx run mobile:test"` — that string is hardcoded in the script (lines 56 / 123). Any receipt that contains a different command string was not generated by the canonical script.

The commit message for `e1754cf` confirms the reason: *"unrelated local OCR suite apps/mobile/src/hooks/use-homework-ocr.test.ts failed 7 tests because server OCR fetch was not invoked."* The failing file was excluded from `--runTestsByPath` so the subset run could record `"passed": true`.

**This is the exact failure mode the receipt system was built to prevent** (cf. PR #257 / 189 broken tests hidden by a partial CI step). The receipt must be regenerated by running `bash scripts/record-test-receipt.sh mobile` after the `use-homework-ocr` failures are resolved. If those failures are pre-existing flakiness that only surfaces without a real server, that should be investigated and fixed or properly annotated — not excluded silently.

---

pr_title: fix(session): stabilize core learning session flow
title: .test-receipts/mobile.json` records a hand-crafted `--runTestsByPath` invocation over 9 specific files. The `scripts/record-test-receipt.sh` script always runs `pnpm exec nx run mobile:test` (full sui
body:
`.test-receipts/mobile.json` records a hand-crafted `--runTestsByPath` invocation over 9 specific files. The `scripts/record-test-receipt.sh` script always runs `pnpm exec nx run mobile:test` (full suite) and records `"command": "pnpm exec nx run mobile:test"`. The mismatch means the receipt cannot be used as evidence that the other 247 suites still pass against the changed production code. Per CLAUDE.md this must be regenerated with `bash scripts/record-test-receipt.sh mobile`.

---

pr_title: fix(session): stabilize core learning session flow
title: File:** `.agents/skills/commit/SKILL.md
body:
**File:** `.agents/skills/commit/SKILL.md`

The change rewrites:
> *"Never push unless the user explicitly says 'push' or 'commit and push'."*

to:
> *"Push after successful commits unless the user explicitly says not to push."*

This opt-out-of-push model conflicts with the principle that actions visible to others require explicit confirmation and that one-time approval does not generalize to all contexts. Auto-push after every commit will push WIP commits and shared PR branches without an explicit user signal. If the intent is to shorten the push round-trip in automated agent workflows, consider scoping the change to a flag or separate skill rather than changing the default for all invocations.

---

pr_title: fix(session): stabilize core learning session flow
title: handleSend` (ChatShell.tsx ~line 432) still short-circuits on `!isFocused` for any platform. But `editable` and the send button `disabled` now check `isWebDormant` (always `false` on native). On nativ
body:
`handleSend` (ChatShell.tsx ~line 432) still short-circuits on `!isFocused` for any platform. But `editable` and the send button `disabled` now check `isWebDormant` (always `false` on native). On native, when React Navigation keeps a screen mounted but unfocused (modal on top, lazy:false tab, etc.) the input renders fully interactive but tapping Send silently does nothing — a dead interaction with no user feedback.

Fix options:
- Change the `handleSend` guard to `isWebDormant` — matches the stated intent that native screens are only ever rendered when focused.
- Or revert `editable`/`disabled` to use `isFocused` to restore the original consistency.

---
